def inversion_count(arr):
    def _merge_sort_and_count(sub_arr):
        if len(sub_arr) <= 1:
            return sub_arr, 0

        mid = len(sub_arr) // 2
        left_half, left_inversions = _merge_sort_and_count(sub_arr[:mid])
        right_half, right_inversions = _merge_sort_and_count(sub_arr[mid:])

        merged_arr = []
        split_inversions = 0
        i, j = 0, 0

        while i < len(left_half) and j < len(right_half):
            if left_half[i] <= right_half[j]:
                merged_arr.append(left_half[i])
                i += 1
            else:
                merged_arr.append(right_half[j])
                split_inversions += (len(left_half) - i)
                j += 1

        merged_arr.extend(left_half[i:])
        merged_arr.extend(right_half[j:])

        total_inversions = left_inversions + right_inversions + split_inversions
        return merged_arr, total_inversions

    _, inversions = _merge_sort_and_count(arr)
    return inversions
